title: stdout pointer corruption via stale binary sign extension
symptom: IR-compiled compiler segfaults on first fprintf call. stdout pointer truncated to 32-bit value by movslq instead of movq.
root_cause: Stale binary from previous build used movslq for 64-bit pointer load. Clean rebuild with correct instruction selection fixes it. The IR lowerer was emitting sign-extending loads for pointer-width operands.
detection_method: GDB: print $rdi showed truncated pointer value (upper 32 bits zeroed). Assembly inspection: movslq where movq required.
fix_summary: Full clean rebuild. Ensured pointer-width loads always use movq, not movslq.
regression_test: Any program calling printf/fprintf through IR-compiled code

title: Phantom callee-save push/pop in body_only mode
symptom: IR-compiled functions crash with corrupted stack. RSP shifted by 16 bytes due to duplicate callee-save push/pop.
root_cause: ir_asm_emit_one_block emitted pushq %rbx; pushq %r12 at function entry even though the AST prologue already saved callee-saved registers via movq to stack slots. The double-save shifted RSP by 16, misaligning all subsequent stack references.
detection_method: Assembly diff between AST and IR output showed extra push/pop instructions. GDB showed RSP 16 bytes lower than expected at function body entry.
fix_summary: Added body_only flag to IRAsmCtx struct. Guarded push/pop emission with !ctx->body_only check.
after_pattern: /* push/pop skipped in body_only mode — AST prologue handles callee saves */
regression_test: Any multi-function program compiled through IR

title: PHI liveness back-edge inversion, CONDBR unconditional copies, serial PHI lost-copy
symptom: Functions with loops crash or produce wrong results. Variables swapped in for-loop — e.g., loop counter used as base address, pointer used as counter.
root_cause: Three interrelated bugs in PHI resolution: (1) Liveness analysis inverted back-edge direction, marking loop-carried variables as dead. (2) CONDBR emitted PHI copies on both branches unconditionally instead of only on the taken edge. (3) Serial PHI copy used rax as sole intermediate — when PHI_A writes to a slot that PHI_B reads, the serial copy corrupts the source value (classic lost-copy problem in SSA destruction).
detection_method: GDB on cc_alloc crash: r12=0xD8 (216 = size parameter) used as base address for cp[i]=0. Assembly showed mov %r12,%rax where r12 held size instead of calloc'd pointer. Same pattern in main(): argv/i swap.
fix_summary: Fixed liveness back-edge direction. Made CONDBR PHI copies conditional on edge taken. Implemented parallel copy via temporary spill slots for multi-PHI blocks.
affected_functions: all functions with loops carrying 2+ variables
after_pattern: /* Parallel copy: spill all PHI sources first, then store to destinations */
regression_test: for (i=0; i<10; i++) sum += i; — must return 45, not hang

title: Stack frame too small for IR spill slots
symptom: Segfault in IR-compiled functions accessing deep spill slots. IR allocates slots up to -1376(%rbp) but AST frame only reserved 912 bytes.
root_cause: IR spill slot formula ir_asm_slot(r) = -8*(r+2) for ~170 virtual registers requires up to 1376 bytes below rbp. The AST prologue only emitted subq $912, %rsp. IR body writes past the allocated frame into unmapped or other function's stack.
detection_method: GDB showed segfault on movq to -1200(%rbp) when frame was only 912 bytes. Calculation: max_vreg * 8 + overhead > allocated frame.
fix_summary: Added pre-scan phase before IR emission: scan all instructions for max register ID, alloca count, and alloca byte sizes. Emit subq $ir_extra, %rsp with exact computed depth.
affected_functions: all IR-compiled functions with >100 virtual registers
before_pattern: subq $912, %rsp  /* AST frame — too small for IR */
after_pattern:
subq $912, %rsp  /* AST frame */
subq $1504, %rsp  /* IR extension: pre-scanned */
regression_test: Compile function with 150+ local expressions through IR

title: OP_CALL alignment counted phantom callee-save pushes
symptom: Function calls in IR-compiled code segfault or produce wrong results due to stack misalignment at call sites.
root_cause: The OP_CALL handler computed stack alignment using n_csave = __builtin_popcount(ctx->used_callee_saved_mask), counting callee-saved registers as stack pushes. In body_only mode, the AST prologue saves these with movq (no RSP change), so the alignment calculation was wrong by n_csave * 8 bytes.
detection_method: Assembly inspection showed unnecessary alignment subq before call instructions. Stack pointer tracking through function body revealed cumulative misalignment.
fix_summary: Set n_csave = ctx->body_only ? 0 : __builtin_popcount(ctx->used_callee_saved_mask) in the OP_CALL handler.
affected_functions: all IR-compiled functions that call other functions

title: AST/IR stack slot collision — parameter overwrite
symptom: First parameter (argc) overwritten by IR spill. GDB shows rdi = 0x2a00007fffffffe8: 42 (test return value) smashed into upper bytes of argv pointer.
root_cause: AST stores argc at -8(%rbp) and argv at -16(%rbp). IR spill formula ir_asm_slot(0) = -8*(0+2) = -16(%rbp) — same offset as argv. IR store of virtual register 0 overwrites argv parameter.
detection_method: GDB: rdi contained 0x2a00007fffffffe8 — 0x2a (42) in upper 32 bits merged with valid stack address. Traced to movl %ecx, (%rax) writing 4 bytes to the 8-byte argv slot.
fix_summary: Added slot_base field to IRAsmCtx. In body_only mode, slot_base = -stack_size. Changed ir_asm_vreg_location to use ctx->slot_base - 8*(r+1), placing IR slots below the AST frame.
before_pattern: ir_asm_slot(r) = -8*(r+2)  /* collides with AST param at -16(%rbp) */
after_pattern: ir_asm_slot(r) = slot_base - 8*(r+1)  /* slot_base = -stack_size, below AST frame */
regression_test: int main(int argc, char **argv) { return argc; } — must return correct argc

title: Pre-scan frame depth computation for alloca
symptom: Functions with alloca (variable-length stack allocation) overflow the IR frame extension because alloca bytes were not counted in the pre-scan.
root_cause: CG-IR-006 fix pre-scanned max register ID but not alloca count or alloca byte sizes. Functions using stack arrays or large local buffers allocated via OP_ALLOCA exceeded the computed frame.
detection_method: Assembly review of pre-scan output showed ir_extra undercount for functions with OP_ALLOCA instructions.
fix_summary: Extended pre-scan to count OP_ALLOCA instructions and sum their imm (byte size) fields. Added alloca_bytes to ir_extra calculation: ir_extra = 8*(max_reg + n_alloca + 8) + alloca_bytes.
affected_functions: functions with stack-allocated arrays or buffers
regression_test: Function with char buf[4096] compiled through IR

title: 4-byte movl for pointer load/store truncation
symptom: Pointer values truncated to 32 bits. 64-bit pointer stored with movl (4 bytes), upper 32 bits left as stale data. Subsequent 8-byte load retrieves corrupted pointer.
root_cause: IR bridge marks some pointer operations with imm=4 (member_size for int fields). The OP_LOAD and OP_STORE handlers used imm to select movl vs movq. For pointer-to-struct-member accesses where the member is int-sized, this produced movl for what should be a full-width pointer operation.
detection_method: grep -c movl.*(%rax) in IR output vs AST output. AST had 0 movl derefs, IR had 47. GDB showed pointers with garbage upper 32 bits.
fix_summary: Changed OP_LOAD and OP_STORE to always use movq regardless of imm field. On x86-64, all register-to-memory transfers are 8 bytes through 64-bit registers.
affected_functions: all IR-compiled functions with pointer load/store
regression_test: Struct with int member accessed through pointer — must not truncate

title: Callee-saved register mismatch between AST and IR
symptom: IR-compiled functions corrupt callee-saved registers. The AST prologue saves registers predicted by allocate_registers(), but the IR linear scan uses different registers. Caller's rbx/r12/r13/r14/r15 get clobbered.
root_cause: The AST register allocator (allocate_registers) predicts which callee-saved regs are needed based on AST liveness analysis. The IR backend's linear scan allocator makes independent decisions. The AST prologue only saves the AST-predicted set, but the IR body uses registers outside that set.
detection_method: Assembly diff showed IR body using r14 but prologue only saving rbx and r12. Caller's r14 corrupted on return.
fix_summary: When IR is active, force all 5 callee-saved registers (rbx, r12, r13, r14, r15) to be saved in the prologue regardless of AST prediction. Wasteful but correct.
regression_test: Function with high register pressure calling other functions

title: 33 dead accessor stubs returning zero — structurally hollow AST
symptom: IR backend builds structurally complete but semantically empty AST. Every node_kind() returns 0, every node_lhs() returns NULL. Optimization passes operate on garbage, producing miscompiled code that crashes or loops infinitely.
root_cause: compiler_passes.c contained 33 static stub functions for AST accessors (node_kind, node_lhs, node_rhs, node_func_name, node_num_args, node_cond, node_body, etc.) that all returned 0/NULL/empty. These were placeholders from early development that were never replaced with real implementations. Since they were static, the linker couldn't resolve them to the real functions in zcc.c.
detection_method: Added fprintf debug to ir bridge: node_kind() returned 0 for every node. Traced to static stubs in compiler_passes.c. grep -c 'return 0' on accessor functions found all 33.
fix_summary: Replaced all 33 static stub definitions with extern declarations, allowing the linker to resolve them to the real implementations in zcc.c.
before_pattern:
static int node_kind(struct Node *n) { return 0; }
static struct Node *node_lhs(struct Node *n) { return NULL; }
after_pattern:
extern int node_kind(struct Node *n);
extern struct Node *node_lhs(struct Node *n);
regression_test: Self-host: IR-compiled compiler must produce identical output to AST-compiled

title: ZND_CALL missing from zcc_node_from_stmt — statement-level calls empty
cwe_name: Numeric Range Comparison Without Minimum Check
symptom: Statement-level function calls (e.g., validate_node(...); fprintf(...);) produce empty call nodes. IR emits callq *%rax through uninitialized vreg 0. Crash: RIP=0x1.
root_cause: zcc_node_from_stmt had no case for ZND_CALL. Statement-level calls (expressions used as statements) fell through to default:break, producing a ZCCNode with kind=ZND_CALL but no func_name, no arguments. The IR lowerer emitted an indirect call through register 0 (uninitialized, contains garbage).
detection_method: GDB: crash at callq *%rax where rax=0x1. Backtrace showed the call originated from a validate_node() statement in codegen. Assembly showed no function name in the call sequence.
fix_summary: Added delegation to zcc_node_from_expr for ZND_CALL case in zcc_node_from_stmt. The expr handler already populates func_name and arguments correctly.
affected_functions: all functions with statement-level calls (most functions)
after_pattern:
case ZND_CALL:
    return zcc_node_from_expr(n);  /* delegate to expr handler */
regression_test: Function containing statement-level printf() or validate_node() calls

title: ZND_ASSIGN missing from zcc_node_from_expr — for-loop increment empty
cwe_name: Numeric Range Comparison Without Minimum Check
symptom: For-loop increment expression (i = i + 1) silently dropped. Loop runs forever because i never increments. IR latch block contains only a branch, no assignment.
root_cause: zcc_node_from_expr had cases for ZND_COMPOUND_ASSIGN and all binary/unary operators, but no case for plain ZND_ASSIGN. Assignment expressions used in for-loop increments (which are expressions, not statements) fell through to default:break. The ZCCNode was created with kind=ZND_ASSIGN but lhs=NULL and rhs=NULL. The lowerer's ZND_ASSIGN handler returned 0 immediately on null lhs.
detection_method: Test suite: for_loop_sum test timed out (infinite loop). Added DIAG_INC debug print: node->inc existed (kind=4=ZND_ASSIGN) but lhs and rhs were both NULL. Traced to missing case in zcc_node_from_expr.
fix_summary: Added ZND_ASSIGN case to zcc_node_from_expr with z->lhs = zcc_node_from_expr(node_lhs(n)) and z->rhs = zcc_node_from_expr(node_rhs(n)). Same pattern as the stmt handler at line 3083.
affected_functions: all functions with for-loop increments using plain assignment (i = i + 1)
regression_test: for (i = 0; i < 10; i = i + 1) sum += i; — must return 45

title: LP64 ABI Trap: uint32_t boundary struct misalignment
cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
symptom: Stage 2 self-hosting compilation fails with ASan complaining about heap-buffer-overflow during `peek_token`. `Compiler *cc` memory is silently corrupted by IR backend manipulation.
root_cause: The ZCC compiler incorrectly treats uint32_t as an 8-byte `unsigned long` on LP64. GCC correctly maps it to 4-bytes. When `PassResult` and `ZCCNode` are instantiated by compiled GCC-backend code and manipulated by ZCC-frontend code, the mismatched offsets cause massive out-of-bounds writes into adjacent heap structures (which happened to be `Compiler *cc`).
detection_method: ASan traceback pointed to `Compiler` heap buffer overflow. Layout of `Compiler` object showed bounds writes exceeding 120 bytes past boundary. Matched offsets to `uint32_t` width differences in boundary structs.
fix_summary: Replaced all `uint32_t` occurrences in `zcc_ast_bridge.h` and `zcc_ast_bridge_zcc.h` with `unsigned int` standard Posix equivalent to guarantee strict 4-byte layouts across both compiler parsing engines.
after_pattern:
unsigned int order[ZCC_PGO_MAX_BLOCKS];
unsigned int num_stmts;
regression_test: Self-hosting ZCC with ASan enabled must compile large input source cleanly

title: Implicit argument type promotion failure (ABI parameter shifting)
symptom: Functions receiving mixed float/int arguments fail with corrupted sizes. Lua fails with "core and library have incompatible numeric types" and a nil table crash in luaL_openlibs.
root_cause: The parser iterated function arguments locally and appended them linearly to the function's parameter evaluation frame without coercing integers to floating-point specifications. This caused a literal (e.g., 504) to be loaded natively into a general-purpose integer register rather than the XMM register mapping declared by the prototype, fatally displacing all subsequently evaluated integer parameters down the System V AMD64 ABI stream.
detection_method: Isolated function calls into a minimal reproduction test (test_call_promo.c). GDB traced the evaluation of integer arguments incorrectly placed into RSI, displacing expected parameters.
fix_summary: Surgically patched direct and indirect parameter processing pathways in part3.c to dynamically fetch prototype definitions and inject an ND_CAST node if the operand natively mismatched the structural prototype.
affected_functions: all function calls mixing floating-point and integer parameters
regression_test: test_call_promo.c — executing a prototyped function with a mismatched integer argument must correctly promote the argument without displacing subsequent parameters.
